text:
Sumidori (スミドリ, Sumidori) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 was one of the personal soldiers of 9 th Prince Halkenburg Hui Guo Rou. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 

Appearance

Sumidori had short fair hair, dark eyes, and a small nose with visible nostrils. As a Royal Bodyguard, he wore a black suit and a tie. 

Personality

Like Halkenburg's other private soldiers, Sumidori was fiercely loyal to the prince. He did not display the slightest hint of regret when he lost his body and did not hesitate to stake his life to test Halkenburg's theory about his ability. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

On the first day of the voyage, 9 th Prince Halkenburg retires from the departure ceremony back to his room, Room 1009, and is horrified to discover that at least eleven of his men, including Sumidori, have collapsed in his room and are seemingly unconscious. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 Halkenburg awakes in his bed several hours later, wondering if what he saw was a dream, but by chance, looks at the back of his left hand and sees a pinion imprinted there. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

On the third day of the voyage, Halkenburg leads his men in a moment of silence for 12 th Prince Momoze. His men stand in a semicircle surrounding Halkenburg; Sumidori stands on Kōbihi's right. The unison of Halkenburg's men in action generates a massive amount of aura, strong enough for 1 st Prince Benjamin to feel from four rooms away in Room 1001 and contact Shikaku, a personal elite soldier of his sent to guard and spy on Halkenburg on the first day, currently terrified while observing the situation for an immediate report on it. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 375 

After the moment of silence ends, Halkenburg begins walking toward Room 1009's entrance. His men ask him where he is going, and unsuccessfully try to convince him not to see King Nasubi outside a banquet as it risks disqualification. His men find themselves unable to say anything in the face of his strong emotions, and follow him wordlessly as he heads to Room 1009's exit. Halkenburg leaves with six men, and instructs the rest of them, including Sumidori, to continue carrying out their duties. 

Halkenburg, accompanied by Shikaku and several other guards, makes his way to Nasubi's residence but is not allowed to enter, and settles for leaving the guards at the VVIP gate a letter with instructions to pass it to him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 He continues trying this for the next few days, and on the sixth day, forces the guards at the VVIP gate at gunpoint to let him in. Halkenburg speaks to Nasubi, and after failing to convince Nasubi to stop the contest, returns to his room, awakened and resolved to win, and equipped with a new ability. Halkenburg returns to Room 1009 and aims his Grimmal: The Dissonance at Shikaku. At the moment Shikaku is hit by Halkenburg's ability, Sumidori, surrounded by aura and standing right behind Halkenburg, collapses simultaneously with Shikaku. Moments later, Shikaku opens his eyes and rises, cloaked in aura. While possessed by Sumidori, Shikaku stands to face Halkenburg, salutes, and asks for orders. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 

On the seventh day of the voyage, The exact time this occurred is ambiguous, and is most likely on Day 6 or 7, but the possibility remains that it occurs on Day 8. While originally surrounded by completely unrelated events confirmed to take place on Day 9, information released in Chapter 404 about the workings of consciousness of those affected by Halkenburg's ability makes it illogical for the complete interaction to happen on Day 9. A wide-angle panel of the Black Whale is also noted in the middle of Halkenburg's scene in Chapter 386, which has been very commonly used throughout the arc to denote that an unspecified amount of time has passed. Halkenburg and his men stand around an unconscious Sumidori, with a machine hooked up to him to monitor his brainwaves. Guerimori reports that Sumidori's body seems to just be sleeping. Furichosefu asks if Sumidori's soul possessed Shikaku's body, and "Shikaku", standing nearby, answers affirmatively, adding that the possession did not feel different from his own body. The men wonder what happened to Shikaku's soul, and Halkenburg suggests four possibilities, one of which being that Sumidori and Shikaku's souls had switched bodies. Halkenburg states that at the moment, there was no way to prove or disprove any theory, and "Shikaku" volunteers that if his body held Shikaku's soul as well, it remained buried in the unconscious. Halkenburg points out that "Sumidori"'s brainwaves changing would confirm that some sol was in his body, and decides that they need to find out how the ability exactly worked. Halkenburg calls for his men's attention, and explains his understanding of why he was given the ability, and the implications that the reason he was given the ability has on what the ability itself can do. "Shikaku" stands on the far right of the group, and the men listen closely to Halkenburg. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 

On the ninth day, Halkenburg's men gather again, to collectively review their investigation and to be briefed on the next steps to take. Halkenburg shares another hypothesis but adds that it was based on a gut feeling he had, since he does not know how Nen works, but that it was necessary to understand the ability. He asks Sumidori if he is willing to help him, and thanks Sumidori with a sorrowful look in his face after Sumidori gladly agrees to do so. "Shikaku" walks over to Room 1007, and presses its doorbell. When one of 7 th Prince Luzurus' guards speaks to him over the intercom, "Shikaku" proclaims Benjamin, and promptly shoots himself in the head. While Benjamin and Luzurus' teams scramble to make sense of the situation, Halkenburg has his men surround "Sumidori", prepared to identify who is inside Sumidori's body and progress their investigation. "Sumidori" wakes up confused, and looks up to see Halkenburg in front of him. 

On the eleventh day, after a Halkenburg-possessed-Balsamilco reports to Benjamin that Halkenburg had vomited and was taken to the hospital on Tier 3, Halkenburg mentally reviews his ability, thinking about how their experiment with Shikaku and Sumidori had helped them confirm what happens when the body of the possessor dies, This is the only piece of evidence suggesting Sumidori's death as it was not shown, but it strongly implies that Sumidori has died. and that only one of the swapped minds can be awake at a time, and the priority of who is awake. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 

Abilities & Powers

Nen

Given that Halkenburg sent his men Shedule and Yuhirai to Kurapika's Nen lessons on the second day with instructions to learn all they can about Nen beasts, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 it is likely that none of Halkenburg's men, including Sumidori, knew anything about Nen beyond what was mentioned by Kurapika over the emergency channel. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 

It is worth noting that Sumidori was seen contributing to the massive aura when Halkenburg and his men unite in a moment of silence for Momoze. This is a sign that Sumidori had been marked by Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast; Kurapika notes that those with the mark of Halkenburg's Guardian Spirit Beast are half-awakened to Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372
